trade.summary: Indonesia does not maintain official diplomatic relations with Israel and imposes restrictions on direct trade. Limited indirect trade occurs through third countries. The Gaza conflict from 2023 onwards severely chilled any existing informal commerce.

diplomatic.summary: Indonesia has never recognized Israel and has no diplomatic relations. The world's largest Muslim-majority country has strong public and political opposition to Israeli policies on Palestine. The 2023-2025 Gaza conflict triggered mass protests in Indonesia and sharp government condemnation. Indonesia recalled its ambassador-level observer from the region and pushed for ICC accountability.
recent_incidents: Indonesia condemned Israeli military operations in Gaza at UN (2023-2025)
Indonesian government suspended informal business contacts post-October 2023

military.summary: No military cooperation. Indonesia refuses arms purchases from Israel and bars Israeli military vessels from Indonesian waters.

financial.summary: No formal financial ties. Indonesian investment regulations discourage Israeli capital flows. Some technology investment occurs through Singapore or other intermediaries.

energy.summary: No bilateral energy trade. Indonesia does not supply energy to Israel and does not source Israeli energy technology directly.

scientific.summary: Minimal academic collaboration. Some individual-level research contacts exist but no institutional cooperation. Indonesian universities have avoided formal partnerships with Israeli institutions.
